title: Smoke-Hot Venezuelan Egg Fritter
description: A shaggy, post-workout Venezuelan dish that's both umami and smoke-hot, featuring eggplant and a Buddhist-temple-friendly recipe that's perfect for a cataplana vessel.
instructions: Heat the oil in the cataplana over medium heat.
Add the eggplant and cook until it starts to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the flour, cornstarch, kosher salt, black pepper, and cumin, and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the feta cheese and stir until it's melted and the mixture is smooth.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
Note: This recipe makes 4-6 fritters, depending on the size you prefer.
